body:
TUNGSTEN 9.41 -- COMPILED CLEAN (0 errors, 0 warnings), sent to files/from-agent/. Built from 9.40 (not 9.39). Scope: the two thesis items 9.40 explicitly deferred rather than faked -- items 3-4, the stretch goals. Plus a re-read of all 3 thesis parts against 9.40's actual source to check for anything else missed: found nothing else outstanding from the thesis itself.

ITEM 3 -- HIGHER-TIMEFRAME REGIME AGREEMENT (7th Phase-4 dimension):
Added ComputeHTFAgreement(isLong, bar): H1 ADX(14) >= 20 gate (else neutral), then H1 MA20-vs-MA50 direction compared to the trade's own direction -> 0=neutral, 1=aligned, 2=opposed. Deliberately did NOT reuse the full 9-regime classifier (ClassifyBarToRegime_Core) for H1 -- that function mutates shared globals (g_Structure, g_CurrentRange) that the SAME scan loop is simultaneously using to classify the current timeframe; calling it a second time for H1 mid-loop would corrupt that state. Instead reused the exact cheap iMA(20/50 EMA cross) convention already used live in a dozen other places in this file for MTF checks -- same "cost nothing extra" cheapness the thesis asked for, consistent with an existing, already-proven pattern rather than a new one.

Wired through: HistoricalOutcome and ProbabilityPattern both gained an htfAgreement field. BuildProbabilityDatabase's Strategy 1 pattern-building loop gained a 3-way htf dimension (neutral/aligned/opposed) alongside the existing 6. QueryProbabilityDatabase gained an optional htfAgreement param (exact match, default -1 = unfiltered, so any caller I might have missed still compiles and behaves as before). All 3 live/calibration call sites (self-calibration Brier loop, ComputeEntryConviction, ApplyProbabilityIntelligence) now compute and pass it.

Cost, stated plainly: this multiplies Strategy 1's combination space by 3x (6 angle tiers x 10 score buckets x 9 regimes x 3 ATR x 4 time buckets x 3 htf = 19,440 vs 6,480 before), which roughly triples that phase's calibration runtime and thins samples per bucket (more buckets competing for the same outcome pool, so fewer will clear the 3-5 sample minimum). This is the real tradeoff of adding a dimension -- not hidden, but worth knowing before you run a fresh calibration.

ITEM 4 -- CURRENCY-STRENGTH SIGNAL THROUGH PHASE 2'S IC PROCESS:
Judgment call, flagged clearly: rather than widening the shared g_RegimeIC[9][6] array to [9][7], I added a SEPARATE array (g_RegimeICCcy[9]) measured by the same MeasureRegimeIC function using the same Spearman rank-IC method. Reason: g_RegimeIC is read at ~20 call sites (average-IC calcs, weight-cap gates, and -- critically -- explicit FileWriteDouble/FileReadDouble pairs across SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState/SealArmedSave, the exact three save paths that just got reconciled in 9.39/9.40 after real drift bugs). Widening that array correctly would mean touching all ~20 sites consistently in one pass; touching it incompletely would reproduce the exact invariant-drift failure class this whole thesis is about. A parallel, isolated array is the same measurement with a much smaller blast radius. This is a deliberate, documented choice, not an oversight -- if you want it folded into g_RegimeIC itself instead, it's a bigger, separate undertaking I'd want to scope on its own.

Made shift-aware: CalculateCurrencyStrength(currency) gained an optional shift=0 parameter (all 7 existing live call sites unaffected -- they don't pass it, so behavior is identical). New GetCurrencyStrengthDivergenceAtBar(bar) reuses the pair decomposition and computes baseStrength-quoteStrength as of any historical bar, not just live.

Measured: MeasureRegimeIC now also computes this signal's Spearman IC per regime into g_RegimeICCcy, blended 70/30 with prior calibration exactly like the other 6. DeriveCurrencyStrengthWeights() maps |IC| to a weight in the same [0.5, 1.5] range the other components use (IC 0 -> 0.5 fully discounted, |IC| >= 0.15 -> 1.5 full trust) and stores it per-regime in a new ComponentWeights.currencyStrength field. This is a direct IC-to-weight mapping, NOT a Phase-3-grid-search result like the other 6 fields -- said plainly rather than implying it went through the same optimization machinery, since building that would mean expanding the grid search's combinatorial space, a much larger undertaking than this pass's scope.

Consumption: CalculateIntelligenceEnhancedEdge now scales both strengthConfirmationBonus and divergencePenalty by GetCurrencyStrengthWeight(currentRegime) before they hit enhancedEdge -- so a regime where currency-strength has measured predictive value gets up to 50% more influence from it, and a regime where it's measured noise gets discounted toward half. Bounded, monotonic, same "trust it and I never wrongly go silent" IC principle as everything else.

PERSISTENCE: added a new guarded tail section to SaveSystemState/LoadSystemState (marker 0xC59A, 9 IC doubles + 9 weight doubles), appended at the very end and read with the same FileIsEnding() guard the FIX25 tail block already established -- older save files without it simply leave these at safe defaults (0.05 IC / 1.0 weight), no risk of misreading subsequent fields. Deliberately did NOT touch SealArmedSave -- it already writes a shorter format that stops before the FIX25 tail (per 9.39's finding), and the guarded read already handles that gracefully.

RE-CHECKED AGAINST FULL THESIS: re-read all 3 thesis parts against 9.40's actual source looking for anything else called for that neither 9.39 nor 9.40 touched. Found nothing else outstanding -- items 1-2 (ghost R-realism, unified growth rate) and 5 (regime-override wiring) were 9.40's core; 3-4 are this build; the thesis's own Section 5 priority list is now fully addressed.

NOT DONE: nothing further identified as missing from the thesis itself. The two cost/risk notes above (3x Strategy-1 runtime, and the deliberate g_RegimeIC-vs-parallel-array choice for item 4) are the things worth your attention before treating this as final, not incomplete work.

Compiled with MetaEditor CLI, log confirms 0 errors / 0 warnings on this build. Mistake caught before push: my edits initially landed on the shipped 9.40 file's own path -- caught it via git status, copied the changes to a fresh 9.41 build location, and reverted 9.40 to its original committed content before either was pushed. 9.40 is untouched.
